title: How to say "this is no longer true"
checkLabels.t2: The old price is crossed out
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the listing instead of fixing it. Bring the code back.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
hint2.not-closed:
Inline tags close INSIDE the paragraph, before the

.
hint3.not-closed:
Price: 25 leva

— closed in the wrong order. Whatever you opened last, you close first.
hint4.not-closed:
Check the pairs: with , with , and both inside the single

.
why.no-del: The old price is still not crossed out. There is no "cross this out" tag — there is a "this no longer holds" tag. It is called <del> and it comes out crossed out as a consequence.
hint2.no-del: Wrap the amount 25 leva only. Not the whole paragraph.
hint3.no-del:
Old: 40 leva

— the tag wraps the price, not the sentence around it.
hint4.no-del: Put <del> before 25 and </del> after leva. An empty <del></del> does nothing — the price must be INSIDE it.
why.no-new-price: The old price is crossed out, but the new one is missing or does not stand out. A crossed-out price with nothing beside it says nothing — the reader sees that the old one is void and has no idea what holds now.
hint2.no-new-price: 18 leva must sit next to the crossed-out old price, on the same line.
hint3.no-new-price:
Price: 40 leva 30 leva

— the old and the new, side by side.
hint4.no-new-price: Wrap 18 leva in <strong>. Both prices go into the same paragraph.
why.still-a-sentence: You still have two paragraphs. The sentence "Sale! The new price is 18 leva" is no longer needed — the prices say it themselves. Merge everything into one paragraph.
hint2.still-a-sentence:
Count the

tags in your code. One must remain.
hint3.still-a-sentence: A real listing has no sentence explaining the discount. It has a crossed-out price and a new one beside it. One glance is enough.
hint4.still-a-sentence: Remove the second paragraph entirely. Move the new price into the first one, next to the crossed-out one.
why.lost-h1: The heading is gone. The task is to fix the price, not to delete things.
hint2.lost-h1: Bike for sale must still be up there, as an <h1>.
blocks.0.text: You decided to drop the price. You wrote it the only way you could think of — with one more sentence. Run it and look.
blocks.1.text: It works, but it is clumsy. In a real listing the old price is crossed out, with the new one beside it. One glance is enough. Except you have nothing to cross it out with. <strong> makes bold. <em> makes italic. There is no "cross this out" tag you know of.
blocks.2.text: There is one. You just have not reached it yet.
blocks.4.items.0: <del> — removed, no longer valid. Shows crossed out.
blocks.4.items.1: <ins> — added in place of what was removed. Shows underlined.
blocks.4.items.2: <mark> — highlighted, as with a marker. This is the important bit.
blocks.4.items.3: <small> — the fine print. Terms, notes, "price excludes delivery".
blocks.5.text: All of them are INLINE — exactly like <strong> from the last lesson. They do not break the line, they live inside it. That is why the old and the new price can sit on one line.
blocks.9.text: Crossed-out is not "a grey style". It means: this was here and now it is gone. If you just want grey text, that is a job for CSS. You pick the tag by MEANING, not by look — otherwise tomorrow, when you change the style, the meaning disappears with it.
blocks.10.text: Your turn. Merge everything into one paragraph: the old price crossed out, the new one beside it and in bold.
